raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Proposition 57 established that juvenile criminals would only be tried as adults by order of a judge after a fitness hearing.",
    "Senate Bill 1391 barred 14- and 15-year-olds from being tried as adults regardless of the crime committed.",
    "Juveniles who commit gruesome crimes are now committed to a juvenile facility and released no later than when they turn 25.",
    "Some juveniles can have their criminal records sealed upon release, allowing dangerous individuals to return to communities without notice.",
    "In Sacramento County in 2017, only six out of 6,249 juvenile cases were recommended to be tried in adult court.",
    "Only 32 youths statewide were tried as adults in the year after Proposition 57 passed.",
    "Senate Republicans and district attorneys warned that SB 1391 was a dangerous bill.",
    "Some juveniles are committing heinous crimes such as murder, arson, and kidnapping.",
    "Reward-based treatment holds some hope for managing teens with antisocial personality disorder, but these conditions cannot be cured.",
    "Changes to ballot measures passed by the people can only be made by another vote of the people."
  ]
}
```
